back_translation: Six days later, Jesus took Peter, James, and John (the younger brother of James) and led them up a high mountain by themselves.
Then His body was transfigured before them. His face shone like the sun, and His garments became white as the light.
Behold, Moses and Elijah appeared to them, conversing with Him.
Peter humbly said to Jesus, "Lord, it is good for us to be here. If You wish, I will build three shelters here—one for You, one for Moses, and one for Elijah."
While Peter was still speaking, behold, a radiant cloud enveloped them, and behold, a voice came from the cloud, declaring, "This is My beloved Son, in whom I am greatly well pleased. Listen to Him!"
When the disciples heard this, they fell prostrate on the ground and were exceedingly afraid.
Jesus came over and touched them, saying, "Arise; do not be afraid."
When they lifted their faces, they saw no one except Jesus alone.
As they came down from the mountain, Jesus strictly commanded them, "Do not tell anyone about this vision until the Son of Man has risen from the dead."
His disciples asked Him, "Then why do the scribes say that Elijah must come first?"
He answered, "Elijah does indeed come, and he will restore all things.
But I tell you that Elijah has already come, yet they did not recognize him but did to him whatever they wished. In the same way, the Son of Man will suffer at their hands."
Then the disciples understood that He had spoken to them concerning John the Baptizer.
When He and His disciples came to the crowd, a man approached Him and knelt before Him.
He pleaded, "Lord, have mercy on my son, for he has epilepsy and suffers greatly. He often falls into the fire and often into the water.
I brought him to Your disciples, but they were unable to heal him."
Jesus answered, "O faithless and perverse generation! How long must I be with you? How long must I endure you? Bring the boy here to Me."
Jesus rebuked the demon, and it came out of the boy, and the boy was fully healed from that moment.
Then the disciples came to Jesus privately and asked, "Why were we unable to cast it out?"

He told them, "Because of your little faith. Truly I tell you, if you have faith like a mustard seed, you will tell this mountain, 'Move from here to there,' and it will move, and nothing will be impossible for you.
While they were gathering together in Galilee, Jesus told them, "The Son of Man is about to be handed over into the hands of men,
and they will kill Him, and on the third day He will rise again." And the disciples were exceedingly grieved.
When He and His disciples arrived in Capernaum, the collectors of the two-drachma tax came to Peter and asked, "Does your Teacher not pay the two-drachma tax?"

Peter answered, "Yes." And when Peter entered the house, Jesus spoke first, saying, "Simon, what do you think? From whom do the kings of the earth collect taxes and tributes—from their own sons or from foreigners?"
When Peter answered, "From foreigners," Jesus said to him, "Then the sons of the king are free from the tax.

But so that we do not cause them to stumble, go to the lake, cast a hook, and take the first fish that comes up. When you open its mouth, you will find a stater coin. Take that coin and give it to them as the tax for Me and for you."
